name: Rust View Types (Work in Progress)
summary: Proposed Rust extension to express partial/disjoint borrows of struct fields in function signatures, eliminating today's spurious inter-procedural borrow conflicts. Syntax &mut {f1, f2} self records multiple loans (one per named field) instead of a single whole-struct loan, so two methods accessing disjoint field sets compose. Niko Matsakis's maximally-minimal design pairs explicit field lists with potential row-polymorphism inference; the competing RFC #3736 (Partial Types) generalizes to nested products. Pre-RFC / design-space discussion as of 2026-04.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**View Types** are a proposed extension to Rust to express partial/disjoint borrows of struct fields in function signatures, eliminating today's spurious inter-procedural borrow conflicts.

The syntax `&mut {f1, f2} self` records *multiple* loans (one per named field) instead of a single whole-struct loan, so two methods accessing disjoint field sets compose. Today, callers can split a struct into disjoint field borrows inside a single function body, but the borrow checker cannot express "this method only borrows fields `a` and `b`" in a function signature, forcing callers either to inline the function or to refactor the data.

sections.title: Design space — max-min vs Partial Types RFC
sections.content: Niko Matsakis's "maximally minimal" design pairs explicit field lists with potential row-polymorphism inference; the competing RFC #3736 ("Partial Types") generalizes to nested products. The two share the goal of letting function signatures describe sub-struct loans rather than whole-struct loans but pick different points on the inference / explicitness curve.

sections.title: Status
sections.content: Status (as of 2026-04): design-space discussion / pre-RFC area rather than a stabilized Rust feature. The maximally-minimal write-up appeared in March 2026; adoption depends on Polonius progress and on consolidating the multiple competing RFC variants.
